segments.text: Brothers and fathers,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: he said,
Please listen as I give my defense before you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: "When they heard him speaking to them in Aramaic, they became very quiet.
I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: he began.
However, I was brought up here in this city, and sat at the feet of Gamaliel. I was taught to strictly observe the law of our fathers. I was zealous for God, just like all of you here today,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: and I persecuted the people of this Way—having them put to death, and imprisoning both men and women.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: As the high priest and the council of the elders can also verify, I received from them letters of authorization addressed to the Jewish brothers in Damascus, and went there to arrest these people and bring them as prisoners to Jerusalem to be punished.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: At around noon, while I was on my way and approaching Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven shone all around me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I fell to the ground, and I heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: ‘Who are you, Lord?’ I answered. ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, the one you are persecuting,’ he told me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Those who were traveling with me did see the light, but they didn't hear the voice that spoke to me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ I asked. The Lord told me, ‘Get up and go into Damascus, and there you'll be told everything that's arranged for you to do.’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Since I couldn't see because of the brightness of the light, those who were with me led me by the hand into Damascus.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: There a man called Ananias came to see me. He was a religious man who observed the law, and was highly respected by the Jews who lived in the town.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: He stood in front of me and said, ‘Brother Saul, receive back your sight.’ At that very moment I could see again, and I looked at him.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: He told me, ‘The God of our fathers has appointed you to know his will, to see the one who is truly good and right, [^95] and to listen to what he has to say to you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: You will testify for him to everyone about what you have seen and heard. So what are you waiting for?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I returned to Jerusalem, and as I was praying in the Temple, I fell into a trance.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I saw a vision of the Lord telling me, ‘Hurry! You need to leave Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept what you are telling them about me.’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I replied, ‘Lord, they certainly know that I went from synagogue to synagogue, beating and imprisoning those who trusted in you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: When Stephen was killed for testifying about you I was standing there in full agreement with those who killed him, holding their coats for them.’
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: The Lord told me, ‘Leave now, for I am sending you far away to the foreigners.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: "Up until this point they had listened to what he was saying, but then they started shouting,
Get rid of this man from the earth—he doesn't deserve to live!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: "They screamed and tore off their coats and threw dust into the air.
"The commander ordered Paul brought into the fortress, and gave orders for him to be interrogated by flogging so he could find out the reason people were shouting so much against Paul.
"As they stretched him out and tied him down to flog him, Paul asked the centurion standing there,
Is it legal to flog a Roman citizen who hasn't had a trial?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: "When the centurion heard what Paul said, he went to the commander and asked him,
What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen.
segments.speaker: Centurion

segments.text: "The commander came, and asked Paul,
Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?
segments.speaker: The commander came, and

segments.text: Paul replied,
Yes I am.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I paid a lot of money to buy Roman citizenship,
segments.speaker: Commander

segments.text: said the commander.
But I was born a citizen,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Paul replied.
"Those who were about to interrogate Paul left immediately. The commander was worried when he found out that Paul was a Roman citizen because he had placed him in chains. [^96]

"The next day, wanting to find out the reason why the Jews were accusing Paul, he had him released and taken before the chief priests and the whole council which he ordered to assemble. He had Paul brought down and placed him before them.
